resources.string: Location permission (including background access) is required. Tap below to open Settings, then choose Permissions → Location → Allow all the time.

Timeline Tracks needs permission to show a notification while it\'s running. Without it, there\'s no way to see that tracking is active or to stop it — you might forget it\'s on.\n\nTap Go to Settings, then enable Notifications for Timeline Tracks.

Timeline Tracks runs in the background, requesting GPS locations at your chosen interval, then discarding them. A side effect is that Google Timeline — and any other app on your phone — receives those locations for free, without needing to request GPS themselves.

This is especially useful when hiking, sailing, or anywhere Wi-Fi location doesn't work well. A shorter interval gives Timeline higher-quality tracks; a longer interval saves battery.

Auto-stop: If 5 consecutive locations are all within 100 meters of each other, the app assumes you're stationary and stops automatically.

Timeline Tracks is 100% privacy-safe: it never stores your locations, runs entirely on your phone, and has no internet permission — so it's physically impossible for it to send your location data anywhere. It's also completely free.
Persistent notification while GPS tracking is active
